ACM SIGCHI

ACM SIGCHI
HomeTimelineIntel

ACM SIGCHI Student Chapter Presents

GRID
LOCK

The War Has Reached a Stalemate.

MISSION BRIEFING

This is GRIDLOCK.

GRIDLOCK is a multi-stage technical adventure challenge that simulates restoring a corrupted digital grid system across campus.

Teams must decode, calculate, and navigate through three power nodes before executing a final GPS-based precision capture at Vendhar Square.

  • codeCompetitive coding
  • psychologyLogical puzzle solving
  • location_onCampus-based coordinate hunt
  • gps_fixedGPS precision-based final capture

Encounter Data

Organized byACM SIGCHI Student Chapter – SRM
ThemePower Rangers
Date30 March
Time9:00 AM – 4:00 PM
Team Size3–4 Members
WinnersTop 3 Teams

All assets must be secured within designated protocol windows.

CODEATHON

Build Under Pressure.

Teams build a working prototype to solve real-world problem statements.

Architect scalable solutions and present your vision to industry judges.

What You'll Be Asked To Do

  • System design
  • Implementation
  • Optimization
  • Presentation

OFFLINE CAPTURE THE FLAG

Break. Decrypt. Extract.

A live, on-ground competition where teams face timed cybersecurity challenges in a LAN-based environment.

Compete on a real-time leaderboard as you extract flags and submit in the correct format.

Flag format submission required for validation

Arena Stats

Duration4 Hours
FormatOffline LAN
Team Size3–4
LeaderboardLive

THE THREE KEYS

Master these to break the stalemate

INTELLIGENCE

Gather data, analyze patterns, exploit weaknesses

DECRYPTION

Break codes, reverse engineer, unlock secrets

CONTROL

Dominate systems, execute commands, claim victory

Event Timeline

Stay on schedule. Don't miss a single objective in this full-day tactical engagement.

9:00 – 9:30

9:00 – 9:30

Registration

Check-in and team verification. Secure your access keys and prepare for deployment.

Check-in Active

9:30 – 10:00

9:30 – 10:00

Opening Brief + Rules

Mission overview, environment setup, and engagement protocols. Understanding the rules is the difference between victory and stalemate.

10:00 – 12:15

10:00 – 12:15

Round 1 – Codathon

The primary construction phase. Architect and implement your core solution to the tactical problems presented.

12:20 – 1:30

12:20 – 1:30

Lunch Break

Strategic refuel. Analyze the first phase performance and recalibrate for the afternoon assault.

1:30 – 2:45

1:30 – 2:45

Round 2 – 3-Node Rotation

High-speed tactical rotation. Coordinate with your team to secure and maintain control over critical network nodes.

2:45 – 3:45

2:45 – 3:45

Round 3 – GPS Precision Capture

Final field engagement. Deploy precision capture techniques to secure geographical assets and tip the scales.

3:45 – 4:00

3:45 – 4:00

Results + Closing

Data finalization and victor announcement. The stalemate is broken.
